target.title: fix(podman): mount host ~/.claude into setup container
target.body:
## Summary
- Setup wizard fails with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` under rootless Podman because `~/.claude` is never mounted into the onboard container, so `readClaudeCliCredentials` cannot find `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json`.
- `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` is intentionally stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`), so the env workaround does not survive managed runs.
- Bind-mount the host's `~/.claude` read-only into the setup container when `.credentials.json` exists. Setup-only — gateway already has the migrated credentials under `$CONFIG_DIR`.
- Uses the shared SELinux relabel (`,z`) instead of the exclusive `,Z` so the host's own Claude CLI keeps access to its credentials after the container exits.
- Allows overriding the host source via `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`.

Fixes #81899

## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed:** Rootless Podman setup wizard run from a host that has a working `~/.claude/.credentials.json` fails at "Anthropic Claude CLI" onboarding with `Claude CLI is not authenticated on this host` (#81899), because `scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh` never mounted the host `.claude` directory into the setup container, and `readClaudeCliCredentials` (`extensions/anthropic/cli-shared.ts`) reads from `/home/node/.claude/.credentials.json` inside the container with `CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR` stripped by `CLAUDE_CLI_CLEAR_ENV`. After this patch the setup container gets a conditional, read-only, shared-SELinux-relabel bind mount of the host `~/.claude` so the existing auth read path succeeds without leaking the credential outside setup or stealing the host CLI's SELinux label.

- **Real environment tested:** macOS 25.3.0 (Darwin arm64), `node v25.9.0`, `pnpm 11.1.0`, real OpenClaw checkout at `/private/tmp/openclaw` rebased onto upstream `openclaw/main@0dc04fb9261`. The patch touches one file (`scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`). Verification is a source-level/script-level repro: `bash -n` syntax check on the patched script, plus a `bash -x` trace of the mount-construction code path with `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR` pointed at a fixture directory containing `.credentials.json`, capturing the resulting `podman` argv. No live Fedora rootless Podman host was available; the load-bearing fact this proves is that the script emits the exact `-v <host-claude>:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` argument that `readClaudeCliCredentials` then reads from inside the container.

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch:**
  1. `git checkout fix/podman-claude-cli-mount`
  2. `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
  3. Create a fixture: `mkdir -p /tmp/podman-claude-fixture && echo '{"primaryApiKey":"sk-ant-FAKE"}' > /tmp/podman-claude-fixture/.credentials.json`
  4. Run the script with mock `podman` on PATH that just `echo`es its argv, with `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR=/tmp/podman-claude-fixture`, capturing the `-v` arguments.
  5. Repeat (3)/(4) without `.credentials.json` and confirm no `.claude` mount argument is emitted.
  6. Diff the same invocation against `main` to confirm pre-fix produces no `.claude` mount.

- **Evidence after fix:** `bash -n` reports zero syntax errors; the captured `podman` argv from the patched script contains a single `-v /tmp/podman-claude-fixture:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` entry (read-only, shared SELinux relabel) inside the setup-only branch, and when the fixture's `.credentials.json` is removed the mount argument is absent — matching the conditional gate. The same mock-`podman` capture against unpatched `main` emits no `.claude` mount in either case, exactly reproducing the missing-mount root cause from #81899. `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR` override resolves to the supplied path and is exported into the `podman run` argv verbatim.

- **Observed result after fix:** The setup container receives a read-only bind mount of the host's `~/.claude` (or `$OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR`) at `/home/node/.claude`, exposing `.credentials.json` to the in-container `readClaudeCliCredentials` read path that the wizard already calls. The mount uses the shared `,z` relabel rather than exclusive `,Z`, so the host's own Claude CLI keeps its SELinux label and remains usable after the container exits. The mount is gated on the host source having a real `.credentials.json` file, so hosts without a Claude CLI install do not see a phantom mount.

- **What was not tested:** No live Fedora rootless Podman invocation was exercised (no Fedora SELinux host available in this environment). The end-to-end "wizard prompts → mount applied → `readClaudeCliCredentials` succeeds → onboarding proceeds" path is the deliberate target of this script change, and is the same path the reporter described as broken in #81899. A maintainer with a Fedora rootless Podman host can confirm by running `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup` and picking "Anthropic Claude CLI"; the diff is a one-file shell-script patch with no schema or migration risk, so the test surface is bounded.

## Test plan
- [x] `bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh`
- [x] Source-level mount-argv capture with mock `podman` on macOS (see Evidence above)
- [ ] Fedora rootless Podman: `./scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh launch setup`, pick Anthropic Claude CLI, verify onboarding proceeds past auth
- [ ] macOS host without `~/.claude/.credentials.json`: setup still launches (mount is conditional) — confirmed via mock-podman capture
- [ ] Host with SELinux enforcing: verify host Claude CLI still works after the container exits (shared relabel, not exclusive)

## AI-assisted disclosure
- [x] AI-assisted (Claude Code, Opus 4.7).
- [x] I understand what the code does.
- [x] Human-run source-level proof from a real checkout against the production script is included above.


## Live Fedora rootless Podman + SELinux proof (2026-05-18)

Captured on a fresh Fedora 43 Cloud Edition VM (DigitalOcean), SELinux enforcing, podman 5.6.2, rootless `tester` user. Real `podman run` against the patched script's mount string, not a mock-only capture.

```

=== ENVIRONMENT ===

OS: Fedora Linux 43 (Cloud Edition)
Linux fedora-vm 6.17.1-300.fc43.x86_64 #1 SMP PREEMPT_DYNAMIC Mon Oct  6 15:37:21 UTC 2025 x86_64 GNU/Linux
SELinux: Enforcing
podman version 5.6.2
user: uid=1000(tester) gid=1000(tester) groups=1000(tester) context=unconfined_u:unconfined_r:unconfined_t:s0-s0:c0.c1023
host: fedora-vm
openclaw SHA: 8447b40aae49b271a9238d70c5f5e58e85e8a405

=== PATCHED SCRIPT — claude mount block (commit 8447b40a) ===

    fi
  fi
else
  SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS="${OPENCLAW_BIND_MOUNT_OPTIONS#:}"
  [[ -n "$SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS" ]] && SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS=",$SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS"
fi

CLAUDE_HOST_DIR="${OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR:-$EFFECTIVE_HOME/.claude}"
CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=()
if [[ -f "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR/.credentials.json" ]]; then
  validate_mount_source_path "claude credentials directory" "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR"
  CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=""
  if [[ -n "$SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS" ]]; then
    CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT=",z"
  fi
  CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=(-v "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR:/home/node/.claude:ro${CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT}")
fi

if [[ "$RUN_SETUP" == true ]]; then
  TOKEN_ENV_FILE="$(create_token_env_file "$ENV_FILE" "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN")"
  podman run --pull="$PODMAN_PULL" --rm -it \
    --init \
    "${USERNS_ARGS[@]}" "${RUN_USER_ARGS[@]}" \
    -e HOME=/home/node -e TERM=xterm-256color -e BROWSER=echo \
    -e NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm \
    -e OPENCLAW_NO_RESPAWN=1 \
    --env-file "$TOKEN_ENV_FILE" \
    -v "$CONFIG_DIR:/home/node/.openclaw:rw${SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS}" \
    -v "$WORKSPACE_DIR:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw${SELINUX_MOUNT_OPTS}" \
    "${CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS[@]}" \
    "$OPENCLAW_IMAGE" \
    node dist/index.js onboard "$@"
  exit 0
fi

TOKEN_ENV_FILE="$(create_token_env_file "$ENV_FILE" "$OPENCLAW_GATEWAY_TOKEN")"

=== STEP 1 — bash -n scripts/run-openclaw-podman.sh ===

[OK] syntax clean (exit 0)

=== STEP 2 — mock-podman argv capture WITH credentials ===

podman ARGV:
  HOME=/home/node
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z
[mock-podman] exit 0 (not running real container)

=== STEP 3 — mock-podman argv capture WITHOUT credentials (mount must NOT appear) ===

podman ARGV:
  HOME=/home/node
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
[mock-podman] exit 0 (not running real container)

=== STEP 4 — host /home/tester/.claude SELinux context BEFORE container run ===

unconfined_u:object_r:user_home_t:s0 /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json

=== STEP 5 — real podman run with PR mount, verify file readable from container ===

time="2026-05-18T20:16:12Z" level=warning msg="The cgroupv2 manager is set to systemd but there is no systemd user session available"
time="2026-05-18T20:16:12Z" level=warning msg="For using systemd, you may need to log in using a user session"
time="2026-05-18T20:16:12Z" level=warning msg="Alternatively, you can enable lingering with: `loginctl enable-linger 1000` (possibly as root)"
time="2026-05-18T20:16:12Z" level=warning msg="Falling back to --cgroup-manager=cgroupfs"
Trying to pull registry.fedoraproject.org/fedora-minimal:latest...
Getting image source signatures
Copying blob sha256:44eadc584088456ee5db7c61d0617dc4080058ffbbb060d077547bebfa237574
Copying config sha256:4a621952e6cc741aee56ac88acb3711d31d82ea892d6114d6ac8c2440eef858b
Writing manifest to image destination
total 4
drwx------. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0            34 May 18 20:16 .
drwxr-xr-t. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0:c448,c703  14 May 18 20:16 ..
-rw-------. 1 root root system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0           471 May 18 20:14 .credentials.json
---
/bin/bash: line 1: jq: command not found
{"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":"sk-ant-oat01-REDACTED
READABLE: yes
time="2026-05-18T20:16:17Z" level=warning msg="Failed to add pause process to systemd sandbox cgroup: dbus: couldn't determine address of session bus"

=== STEP 6 — host SELinux context AFTER container run (z = shared, host CLI keeps access) ===

system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0 /home/tester/.claude/.credentials.json

=== STEP 7 — host can still cat its own credentials post-exit ===

{"claudeAiOauth":{"accessToken":"sk-ant-oat01-REDACTED
[OK] host file readable

=== STEP 8 — counter-test: exclusive Z would steal label; verify ,z is what script uses ===

533:CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=()
540:  CLAUDE_MOUNT_ARGS=(-v "$CLAUDE_HOST_DIR:/home/node/.claude:ro${CLAUDE_SELINUX_OPT}")

=== STEP 9 — OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR override path ===

podman ARGV:
  HOME=/home/node
  NPM_CONFIG_CACHE=/home/node/.openclaw/.npm
  /home/tester/.openclaw:/home/node/.openclaw:rw,Z
  /home/tester/.openclaw/workspace:/home/node/.openclaw/workspace:rw,Z
  /tmp/alt-claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z
[mock-podman] exit 0 (not running real container)

=== DONE — captured at 2026-05-18T20:16:17Z ===


=== STEP 10 — host claude CLI post-container-run ===

2.1.143 (Claude Code)
---
{
  "loggedIn": true,
  "authMethod": "claude.ai",
  "apiProvider": "firstParty",
  "email": "REDACTED@example.com",
  "orgId": "REDACTED-ORG-UUID",
  "orgName": null,
  "subscriptionType": "max"
}
```

**Load-bearing claims confirmed on real Fedora rootless Podman + SELinux enforcing:**

1. Patched script emits `-v /home/tester/.claude:/home/node/.claude:ro,z` argv exactly once, only when `.credentials.json` exists (Step 2 vs Step 3).
2. `OPENCLAW_HOST_CLAUDE_DIR` override propagates verbatim (Step 9).
3. Real podman `,z` mount: container reads `.credentials.json` with `system_u:object_r:container_file_t:s0` label (Step 5, `READABLE: yes`).
4. Counter-test (`,z` shared, not `,Z` exclusive): host SELinux label becomes shared `container_file_t:s0` with **no MCS category** post-exit (Step 6) — host CLI keeps access.
5. Host claude CLI post-container-run: `claude auth status` reports `loggedIn: true` (Step 10) — the exact "host claude auth still works" check from the prior review.

Token + email + org UUID redacted in-place. Mount target IP and hostname redacted to `XX.XX.XX.XX` / `fedora-vm`.
target.author: yetval
